lesson.menu_title: Talking About Clarinha -- Family & Bilingual Moments
lesson.menu_desc: Talk about your family and your granddaughter Clarinha in simple, confident English: describe her (curious, bilingual, proud), use possessives (my, her, our, and Clarinha's), and use warm bilingual phrases you can say to her right now. A real dinner conversation with Marco, an international delegate who asks about your family. Vocabulary: proud, curious, bilingual, encourage, support, grow up, spend time, playground. Expression: 'I am so proud of...' Idiom: 'It runs in the family.' -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 19 -- Talking About Clarinha
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 19 -- Talking About Clarinha

listenings.text: This is my granddaughter, Clarinha. She is three years old, and she is the light of my life. She is a very curious little girl. She asks about everything, all day long. She goes to a bilingual school, so she is learning English and Portuguese at the same time. On weekends, we spend time together. We go to the playground, we read books, and I try to say small things in English. I am learning too, so one day I can talk with her in both languages. I am so proud of her, and I will always support her.

Hello, Maria Clara. This is Clarinha's teacher at the bilingual school. I wanted to send you a short message about your granddaughter. Clarinha is doing very well. She is curious, she is kind, and she loves our English songs. Please continue to encourage her at home. When you spend time with her and say a few words in English, it helps her so much. Children grow up fast, and every small moment counts. You should be very proud. Have a wonderful week.

items.situation: Clarinha shows you a toy car and asks: 'What is this in English?' Answer her warmly.
tips: It's a car! Very good, Clarinha.
Answer simply, with a smile. Keep it short and warm.

items.situation: Clarinha says a new English word for the first time. Praise her.
tips: Good job, sweetheart! I am so proud of you.
Praise the effort, not only the result.

items.situation: A colleague at the dinner asks about your family. Introduce Clarinha in one or two sentences.
tips: I have a granddaughter, Clarinha. She is three, and she is very curious.
Name, then age, then one detail.

items.situation: You want to invite Clarinha to read a book in English together. What do you say?
At the playground, Clarinha points at a dog. Encourage her to say the word in English.
tips: Can you say that in English? Yes, a dog!
Ask first, then help her with the word.

items.situation: Tell a colleague, in one sentence, why English matters to you.
tips: I want to speak English with my granddaughter when she grows up.
Connect the language to someone you love.
